title: Noun ならいざ知らず
titleEn: Expresses 'maybe X is possible, but Y is definitely not'. Often 'let alone...'.
structure: Noun1 + ならいざ知らず + Noun2 (comment)
structureEn: Noun1 + ならいざ知らず + Noun2 (comment)
explanation: Expresses 'maybe X is possible, but Y is definitely not'. Often 'let alone...'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: 「Xならいざ知らず、Yは…」 emphasizes that while X might be possible or acceptable, Y is out of the question or even more difficult. It can be translated as 'I could understand X, but Y is impossible' or 'Maybe X, but definitely not Y.'

title: Noun + ならでは
titleEn: Expresses something unique or characteristic only of that noun; 'only found in', 'only possible with'.
explanation: Expresses something unique or characteristic only of that noun; 'only found in', 'only possible with'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The pattern Noun + ならでは highlights something that is unique or distinctive to that noun, suggesting that only this place, person, or thing could possess or create it.

title: Noun なり Noun なり
titleEn: Lists alternatives of the same kind; 'either A or B', 'A or B, for example'.
structure: Noun + なり + Noun + なり
structureEn: Noun + なり + Noun + なり
explanation: Lists alternatives of the same kind; 'either A or B', 'A or B, for example'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The pattern AなりBなり is used to suggest alternatives or options, usually of the same category. It can be translated as 'whether A or B' or 'A or B, for example'.

title: Noun なりとも
titleEn: Indicates even a small amount or degree; 'at least', 'even a little'.
explanation: Indicates even a small amount or degree; 'at least', 'even a little'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: Noun + なりとも is used to show that even a small amount or degree is valuable or needed. It translates as 'at least' or 'even a little' in English.

title: Noun に Noun を重ねて
titleEn: This expression is used to describe repeated actions or states, 'one after another' or 'pile up'.
structure: Noun に Noun を重ねて
structureEn: Noun に Noun を重ねて
explanation: This expression is used to describe repeated actions or states, 'one after another' or 'pile up'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The structure Noun に Noun を重ねて is used to indicate the repetition of actions or states. In English, this could be translated as 'one after another' or 'pile up'. It's typically used to emphasize a great deal of continuation or accumulation of something.

title: Noun にあっては
titleEn: Indicates a specific situation or environment in which something happens or is the case.
explanation: Indicates a specific situation or environment in which something happens or is the case. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The にあっては grammar point is used after a noun that indicates a specific situation or environment. It expresses the context in which an event takes place, or the circumstances that pertain to the information to be explained or highlighted. It can be translated as 'in', 'at' or 'under' in English.

title: Noun にあるまじき Noun
structure: Noun1 にあるまじき Noun2
structureEn: Noun1 にあるまじき Noun2
explanation: Expresses the idea of 'unfitting', 'unbecoming' or 'inappropriate'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The grammar point Noun にあるまじき Noun is used to express that the noun doesn't fit or is inappropriate in some way for the other noun. It can translate loosely as 'unfitting for', 'unbecoming of' or 'something that must not be for a/ the noun'.

title: Noun にして
titleEn: Expresses the idea 'even for', 'even though', or 'in spite of'.
explanation: Expresses the idea 'even for', 'even though', or 'in spite of'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The grammar point Noun にして expresses a sense of surprise, disbelief or unexpectedness in response to a situation or circumstance. Generally, it is used when the speaker wants to convey that something is unexpected even for someone (noun) or in their situation.

title: Noun にして初めて
titleEn: Expresses 'only when/after... for the first time'.
explanation: Expresses 'only when/after... for the first time'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The phrase Noun にして初めて is used to express a sense of realization or experience that only came about or was felt after reaching that particular stage or status. In English, it could be translated as 'only when/after... for the first time'.

title: Noun にすら
titleEn: Expresses the concept of 'even'; 'even in', 'even as', 'even to'.
explanation: Expresses the concept of 'even'; 'even in', 'even as', 'even to'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The 〜にすら grammar point is used to show surprise that an action or situation applies even to the noun in question. It can be translated into English as 'even in', 'even as', or 'even to'. It's often used with negative or unexpected statements, emphasizing that 'even' in this particular case something can't or doesn't happen.
